channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Staffing Shortages and Policy Changes Impact National Parks and California Communities
item.description: Recent changes in national park staffing and policy, coupled with a government shutdown, have led to a concerning situation impacting California's iconic landscapes and communities. While the parks ap...

item.title: Ottawa River Water Levels Stable, Authorities Warn of Possible Second Flood Peak
item.description: Water levels on the Ottawa River near the capital remain stable following recent rises due to spring runoff and rainfall. Despite current stability, hydrologists and local authorities warn that a seco...

item.title: Pakistan's Emerging Role as Regional Stability Provider
item.description: In these times of global disorder with wars raging in South Asia, Europe, and now the Middle East, force is being used without regard for international law. The latest example was the 40-day war by th...

item.title: Nine Injured in Shooting Near Indiana University, No Suspects Arrested
item.description: Early Sunday morning, nine individuals were injured in a mass shooting near Indiana University in Bloomington, Indiana. The incident occurred shortly after midnight during a celebration following the ...

item.title: Conradie wins flyweight title in thrilling rematch at Undisputed MMA event
item.description: Undisputed MMA’s latest event in Lansdowne proved to be another exciting chapter for grassroots MMA in the Western Cape. The headline fight saw Juan Conradie from TroubleMakers exact revenge on his ri...

item.title: Collins Sichenje Goes Public with Kenyan Girlfriend Njoki Sharon Amid Recovery from Injury
item.description: Kenyan professional footballer Collins Sichenje, a defender for Harambee Stars, has shared a sweet moment with his girlfriend, Njoki Sharon, while in England. This comes shortly after he suffered a ha...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs aim to disrupt Orlando Pirates’ title charge in Soweto derby
item.description: The highly anticipated Soweto derby between Orlando Pirates and Kaizer Chiefs is set to play a decisive role in the Betway Premiership title race. Pirates currently lead the standings on 58 points, cl...

item.title: Concerns Rise as MK Party Questions Integrity of 2024 South African Elections
item.description: A recent article by Professor Bheki Mngomezulu examines the escalating tension between the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) and the Independent Electoral Commission (IEC) in South Africa, stemming from...

item.title: Kenya’s Sabastian Sawe Wins London Marathon in Historic Sub-Two-Hour Time
item.description: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe made history at the 2026 London Marathon by becoming the first person to break the two-hour barrier, finishing with an astonishing time of 1:59:30. This incredible achievement m...

item.title: Advanced perfusion technology keeps organs viable for transplant
item.description: In a groundbreaking development at Melbourne's Austin Hospital, medical teams have successfully kept donor organs alive outside the human body using advanced perfusion technology. This technique invol...

item.title: Ombudsman condemns online leak of investigators’ personal details in plunder case
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman strongly condemned the recent online leak revealing the identities of investigators involved in a high-profile plunder investigation. The agency emphasized that such disclo...

item.title: Fatal Crash in Richmond Hill Leaves One Dead, Three Injured
item.description: A tragic accident occurred early this morning in Richmond Hill, Ontario, when a single vehicle crashed, killing one person and leaving three others in critical condition. Police have confirmed that th...

item.description: Kenyan athlete Sabastian Sawe reportedly delivered a historic performance at the 2026 London Marathon, clocking 1:59:30 to win the race and allegedly becoming the first person to run a marathon in und...

item.title: Zamfara Ex-Commissioner Shot by Bandits While Travelling
item.description: Omo, things dey really tough for Zamfara State! Former Commissioner for Religious Affairs, Abdullahi Maharazu Mafara, almost lost his life on Saturday after bandits attacked him while he was on his wa...

item.title: Recto refutes claim he hindered proposed minimum wage increase in Congress
item.description: Finance Secretary Ralph Recto has denied allegations made by Batangas Representative Leandro Leviste that he blocked efforts to pass a minimum wage increase during the 19th Congress. The issue highlig...

item.title: Buckingham Palace reviews security arrangements for King Charles III's upcoming US visit after White House Correspondents' Dinner incident
item.description: King Charles III is being kept fully informed following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, Buckingham Palace has stated. The armed man breached a checkpoin...

item.title: Goa Police take YouTuber Gautam Khattar on remand over remarks on Saint Francis Xavier
item.description: On Sunday, YouTuber Gautam Khattar was brought to Goa on transit remand following his arrest in Himachal Pradesh, related to his alleged offensive remarks against St Francis Xavier, who is venerated a...

item.title: Alcaraz ruled out of French Open with wrist injury as Sinner eyes career Grand Slam
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz has been ruled out of the upcoming French Open after a wrist injury forced him to withdraw from the clay-court season, dealing a major blow to both his campaign and the tournament itsel...

item.title: TikTok formalises its influence on reading with new UK BookTok bestseller chart
item.description: TikTok’s growing influence on reading habits has been formalised with the launch of the UK’s first official BookTok bestseller list. Compiled using both UK sales figures and data from the #BookTok has...

item.title: Extreme Weather Events Linked to Increased Risk of Heart Disease
item.description: A recent study highlights the growing connection between extreme weather events, such as heatwaves, severe cold, and heavy rainfall, and an increased risk of heart disease, particularly in middle-aged...
